commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a target attribute to the cron Run history card and scrolls that card into view after the History button calls `onLoadRuns`.

PR surface: Source +3. Total +3 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: no. live high-confidence reproduction was run. Source inspection supports the reported path: the History button currently only loads runs, and the Run history card sits below the jobs section where it can fall below the fold.

target.title: fix: scroll Run history card into view on History button click (#59902)
target.body:
## What

Scroll the Run history card into view when clicking the **History** button on a cron job in the `/cron` page.

## Why

Currently, clicking History only calls `onLoadRuns(job.id)` which loads the run data but provides no visual feedback. When there are enough jobs to push the Run history card below the fold, the click appears to do nothing — the user has no idea the data was loaded.

Fixes #59902

## How

1. Added `data-run-history` attribute to the Run history `<section>` card for targeting
2. After `onLoadRuns()`, uses `requestAnimationFrame` + `scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth", block: "start" })` to smoothly scroll the card into viewport

## Changes

- `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts`: 2 targeted edits (attribute + scrollIntoView)

## Testing

- Render `/cron` with enough jobs that the Run history card sits below the fold
- Click History on any job → card should smoothly scroll into view
- Existing behavior (loading runs, filtering) is preserved
